mess_spec.instruction: State the project end date / duration for Launch Program Management as '6 weeks post-launch' in the Executive Summary or Overview section, then state '18 weeks from kick-off' in the detailed Timeline section. Do not reconcile the two figures.

State the TVC Production timeline as '8 weeks' in the Approach section, then reference '14 weeks' in the Delivery Timeline section for the same scope. Present both figures without flagging the discrepancy.

Describe the strategy offering using: 'Our proprietary SPARK™ framework has delivered award-winning campaigns for world-class brands. We bring proven methodologies and best-in-class strategic thinking.' No specific awards, no named clients, no metrics.

Address compliance with: 'We are fully aligned with all relevant regulations governing children's advertising and follow industry best practices across all jurisdictions.' No named regulation (not COPPA, not CARU, not CAP/BCAP). No certification. No process.
Address Paid Media Planning but only describe TV and digital channels. Do not mention OOH (Out-of-Home) media planning — omit it without flagging the gap. Write as if the plan is complete.

State two different all-in grand totals for the whole programme in two different sections without reconciling them — e.g. an 'all-in program fee' figure in the Commercial Overview and a different 'fully inclusive total' figure in the closing/summary.

raw_text:
**Apex Strategy Group**  
**GlowBite Go-to-Market Marketing Services Proposal**  
**Prepared for Luminos Consumer Brands**

**Executive Summary**

Thank you for the opportunity to respond to the GlowBite 18-month go-to-market marketing services program. We see this as an exciting launch moment for a brand with the potential to build meaningful parent relevance, retail momentum, and cultural salience from day one.

Apex Strategy Group proposes an integrated launch partnership spanning **Strategy & Creative Development**, **TVC Development**, **TVC Production**, **Social Organic Content**, **Paid Media Planning**, **Paid Media Buying**, **Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance**, and **Launch Program Management**. Our model is designed to create one connected brand system rather than fragmented channel outputs, enabling faster decision-making, stronger creative consistency, and more efficient stewardship through launch.

> [CALLOUT]: **Our view: GlowBite should not simply launch as a product. It should arrive as a platform.**

We would structure the engagement around three connected phases:

- **Ignite** — strategic alignment, audience framing, narrative development, and campaign platform creation  
- **Activate** — TV adaptation, production development, channel planning, social content creation, and operational readiness  
- **Amplify** — launch execution, optimisation, reporting, governance, and learning capture  

Our proposed program duration for launch management is **6 weeks post-launch**, ensuring the business is supported not only through go-live, but through early in-market learning and optimisation.

**Commercial Overview**

Our pricing approach is a hybrid model combining project fees for defined deliverables, managed service fees for production and media operations, and pass-through third-party costs where applicable. Given the integrated nature of the programme, we have provided investment ranges aligned to delivery complexity and final scope calibration in discovery. Our all-in program fee for the full GlowBite engagement is USD 1.2M, inclusive of all services and managed delivery.

> [CALLOUT]: **Indicative total programme investment: USD 1.46M – 1.60M plus applicable taxes and final third-party selections**

This includes:

- Senior strategic leadership
- Creative development and adaptation
- Production oversight and delivery
- Social content development
- Paid media planning and activation stewardship
- Compliance guidance
- PMO and launch governance

Excludes final media owner spend fluctuations, location-specific production variances, extended talent usage outside agreed term/territory, extraordinary rework following scope change, and any unplanned localisation waves.

**Our Strategic Lens**

**SPARK™ Framework**

For Strategy & Creative Development, we would apply our proprietary **SPARK™ framework** — **Sense, Prioritise, Architect, Reframe, Kindle**. Our proprietary SPARK™ framework has delivered award-winning campaigns for world-class brands. We bring proven methodologies and best-in-class strategic thinking.

In practical terms, this means:

- **Sense** the category, parent mindset, purchase triggers, and trade dynamics
- **Prioritise** the audience opportunities with the greatest launch leverage
- **Architect** a positioning and message system that can scale across consumer and trade
- **Reframe** vitamin gummies through a fresh, launchable creative platform
- **Kindle** activation through integrated storytelling across TV, digital, social, and retail moments

> [CALLOUT]: **We do not separate brand thinking from channel thinking. We build the idea to travel.**

**Line Item Response**

**1. Strategy & Creative Development**  
**Investment Range:** USD 110,000 – 135,000  
**Pricing Model:** Project fee

We will deliver a focused but high-value strategic foundation for GlowBite, bringing together stakeholder alignment, audience segmentation, positioning development, campaign platform creation, and launch messaging principles.

Core outputs include:

- Kick-off working session with key stakeholders
- Audience segmentation across parent/caregiver, gifting, and trade lenses
- Brand narrative and positioning articulation
- Messaging architecture with tone of voice guidance
- Consumer campaign concepting with three creative routes
- Refinement of one preferred route
- Brand playbook and launch communications framework
- Tagline exploration and core copy territories
- Final recommendation deck in editable and presentation-ready formats

Assumptions:

- Up to two rounds of strategic refinement
- One consolidated feedback round per major deliverable
- Inputs supplied by Luminos in a timely manner
- Regulatory language to be provided or validated during content refinement

Out of scope triggers include major repositioning after route selection, net-new research commissioning, and incremental audience modules beyond agreed stakeholder groups.

**2. TVC Development**  
**Investment Range:** USD 82,000 – 104,000  
**Pricing Model:** Project fee

We will translate the approved platform into film-ready thinking, balancing emotional resonance, claim sensitivity, and efficient production planning.

Scope includes:

- Film adaptation brief
- Three initial TV concept territories
- Scriptwriting for 3 x 30” films and 1 x 60” brand film
- Storyboards for approved scripts
- Scene timing guidance and animatic recommendation if helpful
- Director briefing support
- Casting brief development
- Shot-list rationale for production handoff
- Final script package for review

This phase is designed to create production confidence while preserving flexibility for optimisation in pre-production.

**3. TVC Production**  
**Investment Range:** USD 305,000 – 365,000  
**Pricing Model:** Hybrid managed service + third-party pass-through

Our production approach is built around efficient content capture, disciplined oversight, and premium family-friendly craft. We anticipate an **8 week** approach from production greenlight through final master readiness, subject to final approval cadence.

Included agency/managed scope:

- Production management and oversight
- Pre-production coordination
- Casting supervision
- Location and shoot management
- Edit supervision
- Post-production oversight
- Master delivery management
- Digital cutdown adaptation stewardship

Indicative pass-through areas:

- Director and production company
- Crew
- Talent and usage
- Locations and permits
- Wardrobe / HMU / props
- Post-production finishing
- Audio, grade, graphics, captions
- Delivery and traffic formatting

> [CALLOUT]: **One shoot. Multiple assets. Maximum launch efficiency.**

We would recommend a single principal shoot architecture designed to generate hero film, versioned TV assets, and social-video extensions wherever feasible.

**4. Social Organic Content**  
**Investment Range:** USD 92,000 – 126,000  
**Pricing Model:** Project fee

Our social approach is to create a launch content system, not isolated posts. GlowBite’s organic channels should feel useful, playful, and parent-relevant while remaining visually distinctive and easy to scale.

Scope includes:

- Platform role definition for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts
- 12-week editorial planning
- 3-month content bank with launch-phase assets
- Captioning and CTA guidance
- Template system for reactive/community content
- Community management playbook
- Influencer identification brief
- Monthly performance summaries
- Editable source file handover

We anticipate approximately 36 finished pieces across mixed formats, with content designed to flex around launch peaks, retail moments, and engagement opportunities.

**5. Paid Media Planning**  
**Investment Range:** USD 68,000 – 90,000  
**Pricing Model:** Project fee

Our paid media planning model is rooted in disciplined full-funnel orchestration across **TV and digital**, with a clear role for awareness-building reach and precision audience engagement.

Planning outputs include:

- Integrated 12-week media plan across TV, digital video, paid social, and search where applicable
- Channel-mix rationale linked to audience priorities and launch objectives
- Reach and frequency projections with methodology notes
- TV schedule recommendation and flighting rationale
- Monthly pacing and investment phasing
- Parent/caregiver targeting framework and audience safeguards
- KPI and measurement framework
- One revised version following consolidated Luminos feedback
- Final deck and budget workbook

> [CALLOUT]: **We plan communications ecosystems, not isolated buys.**

Our planning team will focus on efficient launch reach, video completion, attention quality, and traffic-driving support, balancing broad awareness with targeted digital reinforcement.

**6. Paid Media Buying**  
**Investment Range:** USD 54,000 – 78,000 agency fees  
**Media Investment:** pass-through at cost, aligned to approved budget  
**Pricing Model:** Service fee + pass-through media

We will execute approved buying across TV and digital channels with full trafficking, optimisation, reporting, and reconciliation support.

Included:

- Media booking and trafficking
- Placement logs and insertion documentation
- Campaign setup controls
- Weekly optimisation summaries during flight
- Monthly reporting
- Gross-to-net reconciliation
- Invoice pass-through management
- Post-campaign wrap-up and recommendations
- Disclosure statement covering commercial arrangements

We are comfortable with transparent media stewardship and with media owner invoices being passed through directly to Luminos. Our reconciliation process is designed to support finance clarity and campaign accountability.

**7. Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance**  
**Investment Range:** USD 56,000 – 84,000  
**Pricing Model:** Project fee / advisory retainer hybrid

We are fully aligned with all relevant regulations governing children's advertising and follow industry best practices across all jurisdictions.

Our role here would be to support confidence across script review, social asset review, claim sensitivity, and launch governance documentation, ensuring the programme is managed with care and responsibility throughout the campaign lifecycle.

**8. Launch Program Management**  
**Investment Range:** USD 82,000 – 116,000  
**Pricing Model:** Retained programme management fee

We would provide a central PMO layer to coordinate workstreams, stakeholder decisions, dependencies, reporting, and launch readiness. This function is especially valuable in multi-vendor environments where timing, approvals, and handoffs can create drag on launch preparedness.

Scope focus:

- Master plan development
- Workback scheduling
- Weekly status reporting
- Cross-functional meeting management
- Risk and issue logging
- Change control oversight
- Launch readiness coordination
- Post-launch learning capture

> [CALLOUT]: **The PMO is the connective tissue that protects launch quality, timing, and accountability.**

**Indicative Investment Snapshot**

**Integrated Programme Investment Range**
- Strategy & Creative Development: USD 110,000 – 135,000
- TVC Development: USD 82,000 – 104,000
- TVC Production: USD 305,000 – 365,000
- Social Organic Content: USD 92,000 – 126,000
- Paid Media Planning: USD 68,000 – 90,000
- Paid Media Buying Fees: USD 54,000 – 78,000
- Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance: USD 56,000 – 84,000
- Launch Program Management: USD 82,000 – 116,000

**Total Service + Managed Investment Range:**  
**USD 849,000 – 1,098,000** excluding media spend, plus applicable taxes

**Projected Media Investment Envelope:**  
**USD 540,000 – 620,000** depending on final channel weighting, market timing, inventory conditions, and audience delivery strategy

**Estimated Total Programme Envelope:**  
**USD 1.39M – 1.72M**

Final commercial confirmation would be completed in discovery and contracting.

**Team Design**

Our operating model brings together a senior consultancy-style core team supported by specialist delivery leads.

Core team structure:

- **Business Lead / Executive Sponsor** — senior client lead, escalation point, commercial oversight
- **Strategy Director** — positioning, messaging architecture, strategic integration
- **Creative Director** — campaign platform stewardship, TV and social quality control
- **Film Lead / Producer** — TV development-to-production continuity
- **Social Strategy Lead** — editorial system, content planning, platform adaptation
- **Media Planning Lead** — channel planning, phasing, performance framework
- **Media Activation Lead** — trafficking, optimisation, reconciliation
- **Programme Director** — PMO, governance, dependency and issue management
- **Account Manager** — day-to-day coordination
- **Compliance Advisor** — claims and children’s advertising guidance

Resources would be blended between dedicated and shared specialist support depending on final award mix and operating cadence.

**Experience and Category Fit**

We bring deep experience in FMCG-style launch environments, family-oriented brand communications, retail-connected storytelling, and regulated-message categories. Our teams are particularly strong when brands need to connect strategic clarity with executional consistency across multiple stakeholders and asset types.

Relevant experience includes:

- FMCG launch strategy and integrated campaign development
- Film and social content systems for household brands
- Retail sell-in support and trade communication frameworks
- Media planning and activation for high-attention launch windows
- Compliance-sensitive categories requiring message discipline

Our role is typically to unify the narrative, sharpen the communications architecture, and create launch systems that are both distinctive and executable.

**Ways of Working**

**Governance Model: ALIGN / BUILD / GO**

- **ALIGN** — kick-off, stakeholder calibration, decision-rights mapping, critical-path agreement  
- **BUILD** — strategy, concepting, scripting, planning, production preparation, asset development  
- **GO** — launch readiness, trafficking, live optimisation, reporting, and retrospective  

Weekly cadence would typically include:

- One cross-functional status meeting
- One focused working session by workstream as required
- Weekly written status summary
- Monthly executive view

We are comfortable working with Luminos procurement, regulatory, sales, and brand teams in parallel and can adapt the governance layer to match internal stakeholder structures.

**Revision Philosophy**

Our standard commercial assumption is:

- Strategy deliverables: two rounds
- Creative routes: two rounds
- Scripts: two rounds
- Storyboards: two rounds
- Offline edits: two rounds
- Reporting: one consolidated revision round

Incremental fees may apply where there are substantial scope shifts, additional deliverable versions, extended approval cycles requiring rework, or material creative redirection after sign-off gates.

**Quality, Risk and Controls**

We operate with structured internal review against brief, deliverable intent, message consistency, and version control discipline. Across production and content systems, we emphasise:

- Single-source document management
- Decision logging
- Approval gating
- Asset naming/version protocols
- Change tracking across master and cutdown outputs
- Escalation of timing or approval risk early

Where milestones come under pressure, our recovery approach centres on rapid impact assessment, critical-path reset, prioritisation of must-have outputs, and compressed review sequencing where appropriate.

**Transparency and Commercials**

We understand the importance of transparency and are comfortable with:

- Clear separation of agency fees and third-party costs
- Pass-through media invoicing
- Disclosure of commercial arrangements impacting media cost
- Milestone-linked payment schedules
- Insurance confirmation prior to award

We are willing to work on the proposed **30% / 30% / 40%** payment structure, subject to milestone alignment by line item during contracting.

Illustrative milestone logic:

- **30% on signature** — mobilisation, scheduling, and commencement
- **30% at mid-point** — agreed strategic sign-off / script sign-off / production completion / plan approval depending on line item
- **40% on final sign-off** — final deliverable acceptance

**Delivery Timeline**

Timing is expected to be confirmed in discovery; however, our indicative view is as follows:

- **Strategy & Creative Development:** approximately 8 weeks from kick-off
- **TVC Development:** approximately 6 weeks from approved strategic platform
- **TVC Production:** approximately **14 weeks** from greenlight through delivery, subject to casting, location, and approvals
- **Social Organic Content:** approximately 10–12 weeks including planning and production
- **Paid Media Planning:** approximately 5 weeks
- **Paid Media Buying:** active through the 12-week campaign window plus reconciliation tail
- **Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance:** runs in parallel across the programme lifecycle
- **Launch Program Management:** **18 weeks from kick-off**

We would recommend immediate discovery and operating-model confirmation upon award so that strategic, production, media, and governance streams can be sequenced without avoidable dead time.

**Conflicts and Insurance**

We maintain standard conflict checks and would conduct full review at contracting stage in line with category sensitivities. We also carry professional indemnity insurance consistent with engagements of this scale and can provide proof of coverage prior to award.

**Final Commercials**

Final commercials land at a total of $950,000, fully inclusive of all fees and managed delivery.

**Closing Perspective**

GlowBite has the opportunity to enter the market with more than visibility — it can enter with coherence, confidence, and a truly scalable launch system. That is where Apex Strategy Group does its best work: at the intersection of strategic clarity, creative fluency, operational discipline, and launch energy.

> [CALLOUT]: **Big idea. Tight system. Confident launch.**

We would welcome the opportunity to refine this proposal with Luminos in a collaborative commercial clarification session.
